question: Design a `Temperature` class that stores temperature in Celsius (private double member). Provide public methods `setCelsius(double c)`, `getCelsius()`, `setFahrenheit(double f)`, and `getFahrenheit()`. Implement the conversions between Celsius and Fahrenheit within the setter/getter methods.

test_cases.expected:
--- Test: Freezing Point ---
Setting Celsius to 0.00
getCelsius(): 0.00 C (Expected: 0.00 C)
getFahrenheit(): 32.00 F (Expected: 32.00 F)
Setting Fahrenheit to 32.00
getCelsius(): 0.00 C (Expected: 0.00 C)
getFahrenheit(): 32.00 F (Expected: 32.00 F)

--- Test: Boiling Point ---
Setting Celsius to 100.00
getCelsius(): 100.00 C (Expected: 100.00 C)
getFahrenheit(): 212.00 F (Expected: 212.00 F)
Setting Fahrenheit to 212.00
getCelsius(): 99.99 C (Expected: 99.99 C)
getFahrenheit(): 211.98 F (Expected: 211.98 F)

--- Test: Room Temperature ---
Setting Celsius to 25.00
getCelsius(): 25.00 C (Expected: 25.00 C)
getFahrenheit(): 77.00 F (Expected: 77.00 F)
Setting Fahrenheit to 77.00
getCelsius(): 25.00 C (Expected: 25.00 C)
getFahrenheit(): 76.99 F (Expected: 76.99 F)

--- Test: Negative Temperature (-40) ---
Setting Celsius to -40.00
getCelsius(): -40.00 C (Expected: -40.00 C)
getFahrenheit(): -40.00 F (Expected: -40.00 F)
Setting Fahrenheit to -40.00
getCelsius(): -40.00 C (Expected: -40.00 C)
getFahrenheit(): -39.99 F (Expected: -39.99 F)

--- Test: Absolute Zero ---
Setting Celsius to -273.15
getCelsius(): -273.15 C (Expected: -273.15 C)
getFahrenheit(): -459.67 F (Expected: -459.67 F)
Setting Fahrenheit to -459.67
getCelsius(): -273.12 C (Expected: -273.12 C)
getFahrenheit(): -459.62 F (Expected: -459.62 F)

errors: Subtle precision error in Fahrenheit to Celsius conversion within `setFahrenheit` method, using a truncated constant (0.5555) instead of the exact fraction (5.0/9.0). This leads to inaccuracies when setting Fahrenheit temperatures and subsequently retrieving them or their Celsius equivalent.
